text: Structural query over every stranger-path break/leak/lie invariant: 'does each have an OPERABLE regression guard wired into CI?' → quickstart rate-limit=YES (smoke P0-1), /api/documents tenant=YES (smoke P0-2), ingest-v2 leak=YES (vitest is_private, Hub Unit Tests), money path answers=YES (smoke P1-1), refusal-cites=YES (#1887 cite-or-refuse smoke) — EXACTLY ONE returns NO: the quickstart vendor-strip (#1858), whose only guard (cp_citation_vendor_relevance) is the inert replay-seam grader D has owned 4 cycles. The NEW code this cycle (#1935 ingest-v2) shipped WITH its own is_private guard, the right pattern — proving the gap is not 'we don't write guards' but 'one specific historical guard was built inert and never activated'. Whole remaining beta risk = activate one seam.

headline: North Star YES on deploy truth @2337a116: zero stranger-reachable live break/leak/lie. All 5 historically-tracked blockers re-verified closed; 6 commits since F5 are all hardening or admin-only (#1935 ingest-v2 preserves #1903 is_private WITH regression test; #1875 blanks refusal citations; #1932 strengthens admin RBAC to capability-based; #1945 label rename; #1928 honest scan UX). Sole residual = the same single unguarded invariant (D's inert replay seam over the #1858 vendor-strip), plus B coverage/detective gaps and doc-hygiene staleness — none stranger-reachable today.
